question.content: Using the three forced-expiration curves, rank Lambda for the lower, middle, and upper curves from largest to smallest based on the ratio FEV1/(FVC-FEV1). Output exactly one order expression token sequence (for example X>Y>Z), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no equality sign, no chained equalities, no sentence, and no extra text. Answer format: a ranking expression such as A>B>C; write only the ranking itself, with no explanatory text, units, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Read the three forced-expiration curves on the same time axis.
step_1.key_point: Use the first-second drop and remaining drop on each curve.

step_3.content: Mark the t=1 slice to separate FEV1 and FVC-FEV1 geometrically.
step_3.key_point: Lambda grows when the early drop proportion is larger.

step_5.content: Compare the three ratios FEV1/(FVC-FEV1) from top, middle, and bottom curves.
step_5.key_point: Bottom curve has the largest ratio, top curve has the smallest.
